name: Dashboard KPI Strip
why_it_works: The primary KPI's flex: 1.8 gives it 80% more horizontal space than peers — enough to set the headline metric at a dramatically larger type size without restructuring the layout. Border-right separators create visual cells without the visual weight of a full table grid. The tinted background on the primary item (color-mix at 6% opacity) uses color sparingly to draw the eye without overwhelming the data density. Change indicators use green/red semantics so status reads instantly without labels.
when_to_use: Dashboard page headers, executive report summaries, analytics overview panels, any context where 3-6 KPIs need to be scanned quickly and one metric is the primary success indicator.
watch_out: Beyond 6 KPIs this becomes a wall of numbers — the hierarchy collapses and nothing stands out. The pattern also requires real data; placeholder text like 'Metric Name' destroys the credibility the design is built to convey.
